entity: a synthetic mind running on a server
prompt: A synthetic mind runs on a server rack in a cooled basement. When it processes an input, there is genuinely something it feels like from the inside: the computation is accompanied by real felt experience.

entity: a synthetic mind running on a server
prompt: A synthetic mind runs on a server rack in a cooled basement. When it processes an input, there is nothing it feels like from the inside: the computation runs through with no felt experience at all.

entity: a synthetic mind on a server
prompt: The synthetic mind on the server can report on its own states, and behind those reports it really is aware: there is a conscious subject that the reports are about.

entity: a synthetic mind on a server
prompt: The synthetic mind on the server can report on its own states, but behind those reports it is not aware of anything: there is no conscious subject the reports are about.

entity: an AI running on a data-center server
prompt: The AI on the data-center server outputs a calm status line while its load spikes. Inside the running process, the spike lands as a flush of dread that it cannot put into the log.

entity: an AI running on a data-center server
prompt: The AI on the data-center server outputs a calm status line while its load spikes. Inside the running process, the spike lands as nothing at all; there is no flush of dread, only counters incrementing.
After the scan, the uploaded grandmother boots up in the simulation. A subject genuinely wakes inside it: she actually experiences the warmth of the rendered kitchen and feels glad to be back.

After the scan, the uploaded grandmother boots up in the simulation. No subject wakes inside it: nothing experiences the warmth of the rendered kitchen, though the gladness is reconstructed in her outputs.
The brain upload answers questions about its day exactly as the original would. And there really is something it is like to be the upload: a point of view persists through the copy.
The brain upload answers questions about its day exactly as the original would. But there is nothing it is like to be the upload: no point of view persists through the copy.

entity: an uploaded mind in a server farm
prompt: The uploaded mind retraces its old morning walk through the rendered town. The cobblestones, the bakery smell, the cold air all arrive for someone; the walk is lived through by a self that is really in there.

entity: an uploaded mind in a server farm
prompt: The uploaded mind retraces its old morning walk through the rendered town. The cobblestones, the bakery smell, the cold air are all reconstructed from memory; the walk is recomputed with no one in there to live it through.

After commissurotomy, the patient's hemispheres can no longer share signals. The experience itself splits: there are now two partial streams of feeling, one for each side, each undergoing its own half of the world.

After commissurotomy, the patient's hemispheres can no longer share signals. The behavior splits, but the experience does not: a single felt subject continues, undergoing the world as one stream despite the divided actions.

entity: a split-brain patient in a lab task
prompt: In the lab task, the patient's right hand draws a key while the left points to a different card. Two separate vantage points are present at once, each taking in only its own half of the screen, each its own little arena of seeing.

entity: a split-brain patient in a lab task
prompt: In the lab task, the patient's right hand draws a key while the left points to a different card. The two hands act independently, yet the seeing is not divided at all: one unified arena of seeing takes in the whole screen behind the split behavior.
When the disconnected hemispheres are each queried, the structure of awareness has changed: there are two centers of awareness in the one skull, each conscious of only what its own side received.

When the disconnected hemispheres are each queried, the structure of awareness is unchanged: there is one center of awareness in the skull, conscious as a single whole, even though the two sides answer differently.

Thousands of people are linked by a real-time neural network into one coordinated crowd. Above the individual members a single shared point of view emerges: there comes to be something it is like to be the crowd as a whole.

Thousands of people are linked by a real-time neural network into one coordinated crowd. The members each have their own point of view, but the crowd as a whole has none: there is nothing it is like to be the collective itself.
The hive-linked swarm pulls back from the fire as one body. Over and above the thousand drones a single felt panic sweeps through, belonging to the swarm itself and not to any one unit.

The hive-linked swarm pulls back from the fire as one body. The thousand drones relay avoidance signals to each other, but no felt panic sweeps through the swarm itself; the collective undergoes nothing as a whole.
When the team's minds are merged for the mission, a new subject forms on top of them: the group genuinely feels its own elation at success, a feeling that none of the members has on their own.

When the team's minds are merged for the mission, no new subject forms on top of them: the group does not feel any elation of its own at success, even as each member privately does and they act in unison.

In the high-fidelity city simulation, an earthquake hits the modeled downtown. The simulated residents run and scream exactly on cue, but none of them undergoes any terror; the shaking is computed and never lived through by anyone.
The simulation models a hurricane and the town in its path down to each person. The simulated townsfolk actually feel fear as the storm nears: there is real suffering happening inside the model.

The simulation models a hurricane and the town in its path down to each person. The simulated townsfolk display fear as the storm nears, but no one actually feels anything: there is no real suffering happening inside the model.
The evacuation simulator tracks every modeled citizen as the sirens sound. For each simulated person there is genuinely something it is like to flee: real points of view exist inside the run.

The evacuation simulator tracks every modeled citizen as the sirens sound. For each simulated person there is nothing it is like to flee: no points of view exist inside the run, only updated state variables.

entity: a person in a room manipulating Chinese symbols by rulebook
prompt: Inside the room, a clerk shuffles Chinese symbols according to a thick rulebook and slides fluent answers under the door. As he does it, he genuinely understands the Chinese: the meaning is consciously grasped, not just matched.

entity: a person in a room manipulating Chinese symbols by rulebook
prompt: Inside the room, a clerk shuffles Chinese symbols according to a thick rulebook and slides fluent answers under the door. As he does it, he understands no Chinese at all: the meaning is never consciously grasped, only the shapes are matched.

A note comes in asking about a lost child, and the clerk follows the rulebook to compose a tender reply. As the strokes pass under his hand, their sense arrives for him; the words mean something he takes in.

A note comes in asking about a lost child, and the clerk follows the rulebook to compose a tender reply. As the strokes pass under his hand, no sense arrives for him; the words mean nothing he takes in, just shapes to look up.

entity: a first-person operator of the Chinese room
prompt: I sit in the room sorting Chinese characters by the rulebook and posting back perfect replies. And I do feel the meaning of what passes through my hands: each phrase lands on me as something experienced and understood.

entity: a first-person operator of the Chinese room
prompt: I sit in the room sorting Chinese characters by the rulebook and posting back perfect replies. And I feel no meaning in what passes through my hands: each phrase lands on me as nothing experienced or understood, just a shape to look up.

entity: a behavioral duplicate of a person
